questionHtml: In some areas there are gaps in the boreal forest where trees fail to grow and peat tends to accumulate. Suggest reasons for this.
markschemeHtml:
a. waterlogged soil/poor drainage
OR
acidic soil
OR
anaerobic conditions/soil ✔

b. organic matter is not «fully» decomposed «leading to peat formation»
OR
decomposers/saprotrophs less active/fewer in cold «temperatures» ✔

meta.title: In some areas there are gaps in the boreal forest where trees fail to grow and peat tends to accumulate. Suggest reasons for this.
